title: identity — broadcast channels
description: Identity publishes NO broadcast channels. The module is internal-only — every state change is either (a) a fire-and-forget event consumed by other server-side modules or (b) a mail / in-app notification routed through the notifications module.
rationale: Real-time notification of password changes / lockouts to open browser sessions is a valid use case, but the correct pattern is: session revocation (Sanctum PAT invalidation), NOT a broadcast channel. When a session is revoked, the SPA's next request receives 401 and reloads the login screen — no push required.
Real-time MFA challenge push (e.g. `Approve login on your phone?`) is an `identity/mfa` concern once WebAuthn conditional UI + push-based mobile MFA ship. That's Wave 2, not this module.
Cross-tab session-revocation coordination (log out one tab, all tabs know) is also `identity/auth` — the auth module owns session lifecycle.
